items.subject: Reminder: Verify Your Email Domain Ownership
items.body_text:
[Salesforce Security]
Product & Service Notification

At Salesforce, we understand that the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of your data is vital to your business, and we take the protection of your data very seriously. 

We’re writing to remind you about the verification requirement for email domain ownership to help maintain safe, high email deliverability. 

Any changes that you already made to prepare for this change can remain in place. Verify your remaining email-sending domains as soon as possible. We have noticed that your organization has recently sent emails from new, unverified email domains. Unless verified, these emails will be dropped. 

What’s Changing? 
To support safe and high deliverability for outbound email, domain verification is required for emails sent from Salesforce. 

Verify your email-sending domains with one of the following methods: 
an active DKIM key, or
a verified Authorized Email Domain.This requirement applies to email sent from Salesforce, including email sent via email relay. Domain-level verification is required in addition to the existing requirement to verify individual email addresses. 

When this requirement is active in your org, delivery fails for emails sent from Salesforce if the email domain–the part after the at (@) symbol–isn’t verified. For more information and the limited exceptions to the domain-level requirement, see Requirements to Send Email from Salesforce in Salesforce Help. 

Implementation Timeline 
Email domain verification is required on a rolling basis starting in March 2026. Salesforce is making every effort to start with orgs with the least volume and variability of email-sending domains. 

In existing orgs, Salesforce generated a temporary allowlist of email-sending domains used over several weeks in January and February 2026. 

These changes took effect in sandboxes starting on March 24, 2026, and take effect in all other orgs, including production, starting on April 13, 2026. 
Verification is required for all email-sending domains added after February 25, 2026, regardless of whether you add the domain to a new or existing org.
Verification is required for existing email-sending domains that aren’t allowlisted.Verification of the allowlisted domains is required: 
In sandboxes: starting on April 14, 2026.
Enforcement of domain verification for all orgs begins rolling out on April 16 for all domains except those on the temporary Allowlist (see next bullet).
In all other orgs, including production with temporarily allowlisted domains, starting on May 4, 2026.For more information, see Mandatory Sending-Email Domain Verification Timeline. 

Required Action 
To continue sending emails from Salesforce without disruption, verify your email-sending domains via one of these methods. With both methods, you make a minor update to your domain’s DNS records to verify ownership. 

You only need ONE of these two options, not both. 
Option 1 (Recommended): Create a DKIM Key. 

An active DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail) key increases your domain’s reputation as a legitimate sender, reducing the chance that your outbound emails end up in the recipient’s spam folder. It also verifies your domain ownership. 

Navigation Path: In Setup, find and select DKIM Keys. Review the list for active keys. 

Instructions: Create a DKIM Key 

Option 2: Authorized Email Domains 

If you don’t want to use a DKIM key, set up an authorized email domain. A verified entry on the Authorized Email Domain list in Setup satisfies the new requirement for email-sending domain verification. 

Navigation Path: In Setup, find and select Authorized Email Domains. Review the list for verified domains. 

Instructions: Verify Your Email-Sending Domains For more information, see Mandatory Sending-Email Domain Verification Timeline. 

If you need more time to verify your email-sending domains, use a substitute email address for unverified domains. 

Additional Resources 

Spring ’26 Release Note: Verify Your Email Domain Ownership
Knowledge Article: Spring ’26 Email-Sending Domain Verification Requirement
Salesforce Help: Requirements to Send Email from Salesforce
Salesforce Help: Create a DKIM Key
Salesforce Help: Verify Your Email-Sending Domains 
Salesforce Help: Check the Verification Status of an Email-Sending Domain
Salesforce Help: Send Email for Users with Unverified Domains We appreciate your trust in us as we continue to make your success and security our top priorities. If you require further assistance, open a case with Salesforce Customer Support via Salesforce Help. 

© 2026 Salesforce, Inc. 
Salesforce Tower, 415 Mission Street, 3rd Floor, San Francisco, CA 94105, United States 
General Inquiries: 415-901-7000 | 1-800-667-6389 
This email was sent to adam.nelson@aptum.com 
Privacy Statement

items.subject: Re: Intro
items.body_text:
[Charles Louis Guerin]
Hi Adam!
We certainly have CMC in common -- you reminded me of many years ago when we made a documentation delivery system for CMC’s tablet EFBs for Jean-Marie Bégis (was he still there when you worked there?) Feels like a lifetime ago.
Would be great to trade notes!  I see you’re running operations, which must be exciting at Aptum! I’ve got my operations hat on at the moment to see how to best get AI plugged in to OBDS to aid our operations (and not scare the staff too much 😊) 

Over the past year I’ve been deep into Claude Code and have watched it mature quite a bit. It went from really annoying to really useful in a very short timeframe.  We used it to build out a full training system for Airbus — delivering SCORM packages through our Aviation Docs platform. That platform now includes a complete LMS now, with grading and the ability for pilots to take courses anywhere, online or offline, and is just deploying now to the training centers.
What made the difference for us was blending traditional senior programmer development with a bit of “vibe coding” - using AI where it accelerates things but still keeping strong engineering control where it mattered. Having senior devs review AI code proved to be a “MUST”.
The part I find most valuable is using AI for “critique passes” - reviewing an entire project’s code against security requirements, best practices, structure, optimization and DevOps. Those checks are fast and surprisingly thorough, which changes how you think about QA. It’s allowed our tiny company to “up its game”
Since January, I’ve been pushing it internally. We’re using Claude Code to build a system to surface all the information that’s usually buried across SharePoint, project boards, time tracking, and quality systems — and make it accessible in a coherent way. Everyone who needs context will now have it, without hunting through 6 systems.
We’re also using AI to lower the barrier for non-developers. For example, our project managers will soon be able to run structured OWASP-style checks on code, follow guided ISO procedures for testing and project initiation, and work through their ISO processes with AI-driven scripts. And we made it seriously a “push button” affair -

Underneath, we’re tying systems together via APIs and MCP-style services, so it behaves like a single environment and it’s amazingly robust. We have it as a desktop app, web app, mcp server where you can define the company’s roadmap, plan projects, specify requirements, define phases and activities, decide whether humans or AI is your best option, enter all the typical ISO information (Risk/Opportunities/Controls/Infrastructure Definitions, Documentation), GDPR, a simple CRM (for now) and the pretty sophisticated  AI Task launcher which can launch from the web or from desktops and do crazy things like – audit every application in the entire company as a batch process.  
Ian mentioned this starts to look a bit like Venta, which is true for the Iso side for sure – we like to save money 😊 … but the intent is to go way further.  The goal is – allow us to concentrate on making our products and service guarantees – and not worry as much about the details.

Hopefully this will let the team focus on delivering features while the system handles the process overhead. I want our staff to be able to step back and operate with a clearer, higher-level view of their role, while AI handles much of the detail work. We spend about 35% of our time testing software over and over – every hotfix, every iteration requires full regressive testing. AI automation for that will cut that down to 10% and free up my staff to concentrate on customer needs, where we need to grow our value.
If you’re doing anything with Claude or Claude Code, I’d be VERY interested to compare notes. I know very few people using it seriously. We’ve got a couple of developers on it now, but adoption is mixed, especially with more experienced programmers (who either don’t trust AI or just want to convince me they’re better than AI). To give you an idea of craziness – we have a Sentry.io 

In any case this ERP system I call “Bienveilla” will roll out across the company in the next few weeks. If you’re doing operations AI, might be possible collaboration to help both our operations succeed.
On Thursday this week, my entire afternoon is free. If you were available any time on Thursday, would love to talk! Next week is fine too, Tuesday or Thursday.
I look forward to meeting you!

All the best!

Charles Guerin, FRAeS
President

Phone:  +1 (450) 476-1420
Fax:      +1 (450) 988-1116
https://ovou.me/cguerin

            Air Safety, Delivered.
Checklists | Aviation Docs | Tech Pubs
    Reliable solutions for safer skies.

BREAKING NEWS: PPI Rises 0.5% in March, Undershoots Expectations 

 

The Producer Price Index (PPI) increased 0.5% month-over-month in March, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics reported, coming in well below the 1.2% consensus estimate and matching February’s revised 0.5% gain. On an annual basis, producer inflation rose 4.0%, also undershooting expectations of 4.7% and accelerating from 3.4% in the prior month.

items.subject: [Event Tomorrow] No Room for Error: Navigating The 2026 Threat Landscape
items.body_text:
[Virtual Intelligence Briefing]
You don't often get email from news@virtualization-online.org. Learn why this is important 

  

The modern threat landscape is evolving at an unprecedented, alarming pace. Attackers are no longer lone actors; they have industrialized their operations, leveraging the power of AI and the inherent complexity of SaaS environments to exploit networks at record speed. 

To help you prepare, join Cloudflare’s elite threat research team, Cloudforce One, for a crucial fireside chat titled No Room for Error: Navigating The 2026 Threat Landscape on April 15, 2026, at 2:00 PM EDT. You’ll hear directly from our experts: Juliette Cash, Director of Cloudforce One at Cloudflare, and Liz Morton, Field CISO at Cloudflare. They’ll explore how to move beyond conventional protection methods and adopt a strategy that addresses today’s most sophisticated attack vectors. 

Leveraging insights from Cloudflare’s network, which protects 20% of the web, we will explore: 

The identity crisis: How infostealers neutralize MFA and turn ransomware into a simple "log-in" event.
SaaS supply chain weaponization: The risks of "connective tissue" vulnerabilities and surgical, multi-tenant breaches.
The AI force multiplier: How generative AI is being used for real-time network mapping and hyper-realistic deepfakes.
The new DDoS baseline: Preparing for the era of autonomous strikes that exceed 30 Tbps.

Register now 

  

Date and time: Wednesday, April 15, 2026 (2:00 PM – 3:00 PM EDT) 

  

  

Copyright © 2026 Cloudflare, Inc.
101 Townsend Street, San Francisco, CA 94107

items.subject: CloudOps | TecEx - Global IT Hardware/Infrastructure Deployments
items.body_text:
[Kian Kinnear]
You don't often get email from kiank@tecex.com. Learn why this is important 

Good day Adam, 

I trust you are well. I am reaching out just to follow up on your availability for a quick 15-minute meeting to discuss the opportunity for TecEx and CloudOps to collaborate. After having done some research, I believe you may be experiencing common challenges we see within the industry, including: 

Turning away potential deals and leaving revenue on the table due to the lack of international deployment capabilities which can be extremely frustrating.
Taking on legal and audit risk when importing into a compliance heavy territory, where one mistake in documentation or classifications exposes you to unnecessary project/ customer relationship risks.
Spending too much time and resources on trying to navigate compliance requirements for different territories instead of focusing on core business operations.
We solve these common challenges in several ways: 

We enable you to win larger deals with your local multinationals by providing the ability to deploy their IT hardware into 200+ destinations globally.
As the importer of record (IOR) we assume all legal and audit risk when importing by placing ourselves as the import on your behalf, mitigating any unnecessary risks.
Our DDP service enables you to focus on your core business while we handle every stage of your deployment process from start to finish.
Please let me know your availability in the upcoming weeks to discuss where there is room for CloudOps and TecEx to collaborate. 

Regards,

Project delays due to customs delays when the nessacery customs compliance documents are not present.

Build Your SUSECON 2026 Agenda: Join our Exclusive Partner Breakfast Sessions
  

SUSECON 2026 is right around the corner - featuring deep-dive technical sessions, hands-on labs, big announcements across Linux, security, AI, edge and a lot more. Maximize your event experience by joining our exclusive partner-only breakfast sessions, April 21-23, 7:45 AM to get a "Partner First" look at our technical roadmap, upcoming program changes, and co-marketing opportunities. Selling SUSE

Capture your share of the $108 billion Google Cloud committed spend by reselling SUSE solutions through the new Marketplace Channel Private Offers (MCPO). This co-sell framework allows you to transact directly through your customers' preferred cloud billing, driving significantly larger deals and deeper multi-year retention. Join the SUSE Cloud Elevate Program and register for the upcoming May 27th webinar to confidently execute your first marketplace deal.
  

Get more details

We are launching SUSE Solutions on Azure: The technical series - your blueprint for building a resilient, secure and manageable stack. In our first edition, we start with the foundation: Management. Learn how to deploy SUSE Multi-Linux Manager 5.x (BYOS) on Azure to centralize patching, automate compliance and orchestrate vulnerability management across 10 or more distributions from a single pane of glass.
  

View the first edition

Streamline your 5G and edge operations with SUSE Telco Cloud on Ampere Platforms, delivering a highly efficient, cloud-native infrastructure. This CPU-based solution eliminates the need for specialized accelerators, significantly reducing your total cost of ownership while maintaining high core density and reliable performance. Explore the new SUSE Technical Reference for a comprehensive guide to architecture, deployment, and tuning validation.
